selection_regression.before: a measured-slow channel was selected by the fourth fresh-affinity assignment
selection_regression.after: 500 of 500 fresh-affinity assignments selected the measured-fast channel
selection_regression.zero_weight_guard: a measured-fast channel with configured weight zero remained unselectable
selection_regression.database_path: the no-memory-cache selector also restricted fresh affinity to the measured-fast channel

interpretation: The dominant delay is upstream first-content latency, not Railway edge forwarding.
A normal weighted probe is unsafe for a fresh affinity key or an affinity failure retry because one slow pick pins the active session for a sliding one-hour TTL.
Warm sessions should not migrate solely for moderate latency because losing prompt cache can add a 20-40 second cold prefill.
The configured priority service tier was not enabled because direct upstream probes were slower on both tested providers.
